---
title: Introduction to Supercomputing 2; Filesystems and parrallel jobs
subtitle: "9:00 - 14:30"
date: "8 June 2026"
author: "(SURF)"
language: 
  title-block-author-single: "Taught by"
  title-block-published: "Date"
categories:
  - HPC
image: ../img/icons8-workshop-96.png
image-alt: "data center"
execute: 
  cache: true
  freeze: true
---

This training will cover the following topics:

## File systems

How to do data management on an HPC cluster and how to use the different file systems in your benefit if you need good performance.

## Embarrassingly Parallel workflows and how to execute them

This module will cover methods for executing many independent jobs in parallel (e.g. using Slurm array jobs,  Background processes, and QCG-PilotJob)


### Prerequisites

- Please bring your own laptop (with an ssh client installed (e.g. MobaXterm)) for the hands-on sessions!
- [Introduction to Bash](./day-1.qmd)
- [Introduction to Supercomputing 1](./day-3.qmd)

<!-- <a href="link" class="button">Register here!</a> -->
